notes: Step 1.6 parser fix: Master cables now load from WIP OR CUTSHEET. Production Masters (e.g. US-SPK03) ship cables in a 30K-row CUTSHEET with no WIP sheet; the parser previously REQUIRED WIP, threw before reading anything, and separately discarded all CUTSHEET rows — so real Masters never loaded (only the clean WIP test file did). Now: require at least one cable sheet (WIP wins if both present, preserving prior behavior byte-for-byte for WIP files); parse cables from the chosen sheet via the same loop (identical column layout, same CAB_RE row-guard that skips section-header/blank rows); tag each cable + stats.cableSource with 'WIP' or 'CUTSHEET'; sheetsParsed reflects the actual source. Removed the old iterate-then-discard CUTSHEET loop (would double-count when CUTSHEET is the source). Added .trim() to _phantom_master_findSheet for trailing-space tab names. Exact whole-name sheet selection unchanged — backup/testing/dated/'Copy of' tabs still cannot be selected. Cables remain in-memory only (session-scoped, not persisted) and are NOT yet displayed — load + host-index + cable-parse only; cable display is a later step. Verified against the real US-SPK03 Master: loads without throwing, 28264 cables tagged CUTSHEET, 4143 hosts, 511 distinct cabs (296 host-bearing + ~215 cabling-only: patch panels, OOB, spine/leaf), WIP-present path byte-identical (synthetic both-sheets regression). JS parse OK (both inline scripts), CSS balanced, three stamps aligned at v1.6.60.
